PLK.description:
Plugin License Key - Issued license for plugin access
Uses ECIES: both parties derive symmetric key via X25519 ECDH
Key derivation: X25519(private, peer_public) → HKDF-SHA256 → AES-256-GCM

PLUGIN_ID.description: Plugin ID this license is for

ISSUER_PUBKEY.description:
Issuer's X25519 public key (32 bytes)
Used with licensee's private key to derive shared secret via ECDH

ISSUED_AT.description: Unix timestamp when license was issued

VALID_FROM.description: Unix timestamp when license becomes valid

EXPIRES_AT.description: Unix timestamp when license expires (0 = never expires)

ISSUER_PEER_ID.description: Peer ID of the license issuer (OrbPro)

SIGNATURE.description: Ed25519 signature from issuer over all fields (except SIGNATURE)

ESL.description:
Entity / Standards Link — join-table row connecting a host-local entity
metadata record to a standards record index row, enabling cascade-safe
mutation and cross-plugin query ownership.

LINK_KEY.description: Stable local link key for one entity / record association.

ENTITY_ID.description: Stable host-local entity id on the entity side of the association.

ENTITY_RECORD_KEY.description: Stable local entity metadata record key (for example `ENTITY:<id>`).

ENTITY_ROWID.description: FlatSQL row id for the linked entity metadata row when known.

RECORD_KEY.description: Stable local standards record key on the record side of the association.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: SDS schema family for the linked record (`OMM`, `CAT`, `OEM`, `CDM`, etc.).

RECORD_ROWID.description: FlatSQL row id for the linked standards record row when known.

CASCADE_DELETE.description: Whether entity deletion should cascade to the linked standards record.

UPDATED_AT_MS.description: Milliseconds since epoch when the association was last refreshed.

PUBLIC_KEY.description: Public part of the cryptographic key, in hexidecimal format

PRIVATE_KEY.description: Private part of the cryptographic key in hexidecimal format, should be kept secret

KEY_ADDRESS.description: Address generated from the cryptographic key

ADDRESS_TYPE.description: Type of the address generated from the cryptographic key

KEY_TYPE.description: Type of the cryptographic key (signing or encryption)

REGION.description: Region of the address (e.g., state or province)

LOCALITY.description: Locality of the address (e.g., city or town)

ChainProof.description: Proves a blockchain key derives from the same HD wallet as the signing key

SIGNED_PAYLOAD.description: The canonical payload that was signed (hex-encoded)

LEGAL_NAME.description: Common name of the entity (person or organization)

FAMILY_NAME.description: Family name or surname of the person

GIVEN_NAME.description: Given name or first name of the person

ADDITIONAL_NAME.description: Additional name or middle name of the person

HONORIFIC_PREFIX.description: Honorific prefix preceding the person's name (e.g., Mr., Dr.)

HONORIFIC_SUFFIX.description: Honorific suffix following the person's name (e.g., Jr., Sr.)

KEYS.description: Cryptographic keys associated with the entity

MULTIFORMAT_ADDRESS.description: Multiformat addresses associated with the entity

SIGNATURE.description: Ed25519 signature over canonical EPM content (hex), signed by the first signing key in KEYS

SIGNATURE_TIMESTAMP.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the EPM was signed

CHAIN_PROOFS.description: Chain binding proofs linking blockchain keys to the same HD wallet

ENTITY_TYPE.description: Type of entity represented by this profile

CONE.description: Cone with apex at origin, +Z boresight.

SUBTRACTION.description: Subtraction (A - B) — inside A but not B.

SMOOTH_UNION.description: Smooth union with a blend radius.

sdfResult.description: Result of a single SDF point-in-volume query (24 bytes, 8-byte aligned).

sdfTimeRangeResult.description: Result of a time-range SDF query (40 bytes, 8-byte aligned).

ENTRY_TIME.description: Julian date when the entity entered the volume (0 == never entered).

EXIT_TIME.description: Julian date when the entity exited the volume (0 == still inside).

MIN_DISTANCE.description: Most negative signed distance (closest approach to center).

INNER_HALF_ANGLE.description: Inner half-angle for hollow cones (0 == solid).

FLAT_CAP.description: Use a flat cap (true) or a spherical cap (false).

INNER_RADIUS.description: Inner radius for hollow sphere (0 == solid).

DIRECTIONS.description: Unit vectors defining the boundary packed as [x0, y0, z0, x1, y1, z1, ...].

SUBTRACTION.description: Subtraction (A - B) — inside A but not B.

SMOOTH_UNION.description: Smooth union with a blend radius.

PRIMITIVE.description: Root primitive type (useful when COMPONENTS[] is empty).

CONE.description: Cone with apex at origin, +Z boresight.

COMPONENTS.description: One or more components that may be combined via COMPOSITIONS[].

ENTITY_IDS_OFFSET.description: Entity-ids buffer offset corresponding to each point (optional).

SDF.description:
Signed Distance Field envelope message — carries a configuration, a batch
request, a batch response, or single / time-range result records.

KEY_ID.description: Logical key identifier used across publication and grant records.

SLOT_ID.description: Host-managed key slot or key handle identifier.

ROLE.description: Role the referenced key fulfills for the module flow.

ALGORITHM.description: Algorithm or key family for the referenced key.

VERSION.description: Logical version of the referenced key.

EXPIRES_AT.description: Expiration time in unix milliseconds, or 0 if unbounded.

HOST_MANAGED.description: True when the secret material remains in the host key slot.

SHWUniform.description: Uniform descriptor — name + type + optional default value.

UNIFORM_DEFAULT_VALUE.description: Optional default value encoded as bytes in the native type layout.

SHWCompileRequest.description:
Compile request — one shader source (typically a fragment of a
larger stage) plus metadata for the host splice.

SHADER_NAME.description: Logical shader name (used in logs and uniform prefixing).

SHADER_INJECTION_POINT.description: Intended injection point in the host pipeline.

SHADER_UNIFORMS.description: Expected uniforms declared by the source.

COMPILED_BINARY.description: Optional compiled binary (if the compiler emits one).

LINKED_UNIFORMS.description: Echo of the uniforms the compiler actually linked.

SHW.description:
Shader Wire envelope — carries either a compile request or a compile
response across the runtime boundary.

PPEPositionRecord.description:
A single time-segment record of polynomial coefficients for Cartesian position
(and optionally velocity). Coefficients are stored per axis.

To evaluate position at time t within this record's validity window:
1. Compute normalized time: tau = (t - EPOCH_MID) / EPOCH_HALF_SPAN
where tau is in [-1, +1].
2. Evaluate the polynomial basis of degree (NUM_COEFFICIENTS - 1) using
the coefficients for each axis.

Velocity coefficients, if present, follow the same evaluation procedure.
If HAS_VELOCITY_COEFFICIENTS is false, velocity can be obtained by
analytically differentiating the position polynomial.

EPOCH_MID.description:
Midpoint epoch of this record's validity window (ISO 8601 UTC or TDB).
Together with EPOCH_HALF_SPAN, defines the time interval:
[EPOCH_MID - EPOCH_HALF_SPAN, EPOCH_MID + EPOCH_HALF_SPAN]

EPOCH_HALF_SPAN.description:
Half-span of the validity window in seconds.
The full span is 2 * EPOCH_HALF_SPAN seconds centered on EPOCH_MID.

NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Number of polynomial coefficients per axis.
The polynomial degree is (NUM_COEFFICIENTS - 1).
Typical values: 8-32 for high-fidelity ephemeris fits.

BASIS_TYPE.description: Polynomial basis type for interpreting the coefficient arrays.

CHEBYSHEV.description: Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind T_n(x). Most common for ephemeris fitting due to minimax approximation properties.

LEGENDRE.description: Legendre polynomials P_n(x). Orthogonal on [-1, 1] with unit weight function.

HERMITE.description: Hermite interpolating polynomials. Matches both function values and derivatives at nodes.

LAGRANGE.description: Lagrange interpolating polynomials. Exact interpolation through specified nodes.

POWER_SERIES.description: Standard power series (monomial basis): c0 + c1*x + c2*x^2 + ...

POS_COEFF_X.description:
Position coefficients for X-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS. Ordered c0, c1, ..., c_{N-1}.

POS_COEFF_Y.description:
Position coefficients for Y-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

POS_COEFF_Z.description:
Position coefficients for Z-axis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

HAS_VELOCITY_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Whether explicit velocity coefficients are provided.
If false, velocity should be derived by differentiating the position polynomial.

VEL_COEFF_X.description:
Velocity coefficients for X-axis (km/s). Optional.
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS if present.

MAX_POSITION_RESIDUAL.description: Maximum position fit residual over this segment (km). Optional quality metric.

RMS_POSITION_RESIDUAL.description: RMS position fit residual over this segment (km). Optional quality metric.

PPEOrbitalElementRecord.description:
A single time-segment record of polynomial coefficients for classical orbital elements.

The six classical elements are:
1. SMA or R_PERIAPSIS (size/shape) — see SIZE_SHAPE_TYPE
2. Eccentricity (dimensionless)
3. Inclination (degrees)
4. Right Ascension of Ascending Node / RAAN (degrees)
5. Argument of Periapsis (degrees)
6. Anomaly (degrees) — see ANOMALY_TYPE

Evaluation follows the same normalized-time procedure as PPEPositionRecord.

EPOCH_MID.description: Midpoint epoch of this record's validity window (ISO 8601 UTC or TDB).

EPOCH_HALF_SPAN.description: Half-span of the validity window in seconds.

NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description: Number of polynomial coefficients per element.

BASIS_TYPE.description: Polynomial basis type for interpreting the coefficient arrays.

CHEBYSHEV.description: Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind T_n(x). Most common for ephemeris fitting due to minimax approximation properties.

LEGENDRE.description: Legendre polynomials P_n(x). Orthogonal on [-1, 1] with unit weight function.

HERMITE.description: Hermite interpolating polynomials. Matches both function values and derivatives at nodes.

LAGRANGE.description: Lagrange interpolating polynomials. Exact interpolation through specified nodes.

POWER_SERIES.description: Standard power series (monomial basis): c0 + c1*x + c2*x^2 + ...

SIZE_SHAPE_TYPE.description: Parameterization of the first orbital element (SMA vs R_PERIAPSIS).

SMA.description: Semi-major axis (km). Standard for elliptical orbits.

R_PERIAPSIS.description: Radius of periapsis (km). Preferred for hyperbolic orbits.

ANOMALY_TYPE.description: Anomaly type for the sixth orbital element.

MEAN_ANOMALY.description: Mean anomaly (linear in time for Keplerian motion).

ECCENTRIC_ANOMALY.description: Eccentric anomaly (auxiliary angle in the Kepler equation).

COEFF_SIZE_SHAPE.description:
Coefficients for SMA or radius of periapsis (km).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ECCENTRICITY.description:
Coefficients for eccentricity (dimensionless).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_INCLINATION.description:
Coefficients for inclination (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_RAAN.description:
Coefficients for RAAN (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ARG_PERIAPSIS.description:
Coefficients for argument of periapsis (degrees).
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

COEFF_ANOMALY.description:
Coefficients for anomaly (degrees). See ANOMALY_TYPE for interpretation.
Length must equal NUM_COEFFICIENTS.

MAX_ELEMENT_RESIDUAL.description:
Maximum element fit residual over this segment. Optional quality metric.
Units depend on the element (km for SMA, degrees for angles, dimensionless for ecc).

RMS_ELEMENT_RESIDUAL.description: RMS element fit residual over this segment. Optional quality metric.

PPE.description:
Polynomial Ephemeris — top-level message containing metadata and
one or more polynomial coefficient records for a single space object.

A PPE message may contain position records, orbital element records, or both.
Records should be ordered chronologically by EPOCH_MID and should collectively
cover the time span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME] without gaps.

CENTER_NAME.description: Origin of the reference frame (e.g., EARTH, MOON, MARS).

TIME_SYSTEM.description: Time system used for all epochs in this message.

SCLK.description: Spacecraft Clock (receiver) (requires rules for interpretation in ICD)

START_TIME.description: Start of the total time span covered by this ephemeris (ISO 8601).

STOP_TIME.description: End of the total time span covered by this ephemeris (ISO 8601).

DEFAULT_BASIS_TYPE.description:
Default polynomial basis type for all records in this message.
Individual records may override this with their own BASIS_TYPE field.

CHEBYSHEV.description: Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind T_n(x). Most common for ephemeris fitting due to minimax approximation properties.

LEGENDRE.description: Legendre polynomials P_n(x). Orthogonal on [-1, 1] with unit weight function.

HERMITE.description: Hermite interpolating polynomials. Matches both function values and derivatives at nodes.

LAGRANGE.description: Lagrange interpolating polynomials. Exact interpolation through specified nodes.

POWER_SERIES.description: Standard power series (monomial basis): c0 + c1*x + c2*x^2 + ...

POSITION_RECORDS.description:
Array of position polynomial records.
Each record covers a time segment; together they span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME].

ORBITAL_ELEMENT_RECORDS.description:
Array of orbital element polynomial records.
Each record covers a time segment; together they span [START_TIME, STOP_TIME].

EPHEMERIS_SOURCE.description: Generating ephemeris source (e.g., "JPL DE440", "HPOP v2.1", "Basilisk chebyPosEphem").

NOMINAL_SEGMENT_SPAN.description:
Fit span in seconds used to generate each polynomial segment.
Informational; actual spans are in individual records.

NOMINAL_NUM_COEFFICIENTS.description:
Nominal number of coefficients per segment.
Informational; actual counts are in individual records.

SNWBatchRequest.description:
Batch target detection request.

Sensor position and orientation are passed inline (not via an arena
offset) because they are small and uniform across a batch. The target
stream lives at INPUT_OFFSET as a tightly packed array of `targetState`
structs from SDR; the output detection stream is written to
OUTPUT_OFFSET as a tightly packed array of SDR `detectionResult` structs.

SENSOR_ID.description: Sensor-configuration identifier assigned during sensor registration.

SENSOR_ORIENT_W.description: Sensor orientation as a unit quaternion (W, X, Y, Z).

INPUT_OFFSET.description: Byte offset in the arena where the targetState[] input stream begins.

TARGET_COUNT.description: Number of target records at INPUT_OFFSET.

OUTPUT_OFFSET.description:
Byte offset in the arena where the detectionResult[] output stream
will be written.

EPOCH.description: Current time as a Julian date.

COUNT.description: Number of detection records written to OUTPUT_OFFSET.

DETECTED_COUNT.description: Number of targets whose SNR met the detection threshold.

OUTPUT_OFFSET.description: Byte offset where the detectionResult[] stream starts.

ERROR_MESSAGE.description: Optional error message when ERROR_CODE != OK.

PRWInit.description:
Propagator initialization request — assigns TLE / OMM / Keplerian /
polynomial inputs to entity handles.

The actual per-entity source record is carried inline as raw bytes
tagged with SOURCE_KIND and, where useful, a SDS file_identifier.
Callers encode `OMM`, `OCM`, or a one-off Keplerian set and pass the
bytes verbatim; the propagator uses SOURCE_KIND to decide how to
consume them.

PRWBatchRequest.description:
Batch propagation request — propagate every entity in ENTITY_HANDLES[] to
EPOCH and write the result StateVector stream to OUTPUT_OFFSET in the
shared arena.

EPOCH.description: Target epoch as a Julian date (TIME_SYSTEM is configured on the host).

ENTITY_HANDLES.description: Entity handles to propagate (empty = all initialized entities).

OUTPUT_OFFSET.description: Output buffer offset in the arena where the StateVector stream begins.

MAX_COUNT.description: Maximum entities to process in this call (0 = unbounded).

TARGET_FRAME.description:
Target reference frame for the output state stream. Matches enum
values in SDS `RFM`. If zero, the propagator chooses its native frame.

PRWBatchResponse.description:
Batch propagation response header — describes the stream written to
OUTPUT_OFFSET and reports errors. The stream itself is
`STATE_VECTOR_SIZE`-tuple rows in the SDS `OCM` STATE_DATA layout.

OUTPUT_OFFSET.description: Offset in the arena where the state-vector stream begins.

STATE_VECTOR_SIZE.description: Components per state vector (6 = PV, 9 = PVA). Mirrors OCM.STATE_VECTOR_SIZE.

REFERENCE_FRAME.description: Reference frame for the stream (SDS `RFM`-compatible string tag).

FLAGS.description: Per-entity status flags (same cardinality as COUNT).
